coredata.dc:title: Selective and ultrasensitive detection of ciprofloxacin in milk using a photoelectrochemical aptasensor based on Ti3C2/Bi4VO8Br/TiO2 nanocomposite
coredata.dc:creator: You, Fuheng
Wen, Zuorui
Yuan, Ruishuang
Ding, Lijun
Wei, Jie
Qian, Jing
Long, Lingliang
Wang, Kun
coredata.dc:description: It is important to develop a selective and ultrasensitive detection of ciprofloxacin (CIP), owing to the relationship between milk security and human health. Herein, Ti3C2/Bi4VO8Br/TiO2 nanocomposites with new strategy were prepared. Based on the heterojunction formed between Bi4VO8Br and TiO2, the photogenerated electrons and holes complete the first step of transfer. Under the action of high conductivity Ti3C2, the electrons separated from the first step are further transferred to ITO electrode. The photocurrent intensity of Ti3C2/Bi4VO8Br/TiO2 nanocomposite was about 10 times that of the Ti3C2/Bi4VO8Br and 90 times that of Bi4VO8Br. The constructed PEC aptasensor presented a “on-off-on” detection signal and completed the specific detection of CIP in milk. With the increase of target detection concentration, the PEC aptasensor presented a detection range of 1–1500 nM and a detection limit of 0.3 nM (S/N = 3). The results are consistent with those of the national standard method.
coredata.dcterms:subject: Ciprofloxacin
PEC aptasensor
Ti3C2/Bi4VO8Br/TiO2
Milk

Analytical performance of the PEC aptasensor
Selectivity and stability of the PEC aptasensor

ce:caption.ce:simple-para: The XPS spectra of Ti3C2/Bi4VO8Br/TiO2 nanocomposite: (A) Bi 4f, (B) Br 3d, (C) C 1s, (D) O 1s (E) Ti 2p and (F) V 2p.

(A) XRD patterns of Ti3C2/Bi4VO8Br/TiO2 and Ti3C2/TiO2 and (B) Photocurrent response and stability test in PBS (0.1 M, pH 7.4) at bias potential of 0 V of Ti3C2/Bi4VO8Br/TiO2 (a), Ti3C2/TiO2 (b), Ti3C2/Bi4VO8Br (c), Ti3C2 (d) and Bi4VO8Br (e) in 600 s.
(A) UV–vis diffuse reflectance spectra and (B) plot of (Ahʋ)2 versus the energy (hʋ) for the band gap energy of Ti3C2/Bi4VO8Br/TiO2 (a), Bi4VO8Br/Ti3C2 (b) and Bi4VO8Br (c).

(A) Photocurrent responses in PBS (0.1 M, pH 7.4) at bias potential of 0 V and (B) EIS spectra in 0.1 M KCl solution containing 5 mM [Fe(CN)6]3−/4− of Ti3C2/Bi4VO8Br/TiO2/ITO (a), aptamer/Ti3C2/Bi4VO8Br/TiO2/ITO (b) and CIP/aptamer/Ti3C2/Bi4VO8Br/TiO2/ITO (c). (C) Effects of the aptamer concentration and (D) incubation time of CIP with the aptamer on the photocurrent response of the aptasensor.

(A) Photocurrent responses of the aptamer/Ti3C2/Bi4VO8Br/TiO2/ITO at different concentrations of CIP in PBS (0.1 M, pH 7.4) at bias potential of 0 V: (a → h: 1 → 1500 nM). (B) The corresponding linear calibration curve for CIP determination. (C) Selectivity and (D) stability of the PEC sensor based on Ti3C2/Bi4VO8Br/TiO2 for CIP detection.
Schematic illustration of PEC aptasensor for detecting CIP based on Ti3C2/Bi4VO8Br/TiO2 nanocomposite.

head.ce:title: Selective and ultrasensitive detection of ciprofloxacin in milk using a photoelectrochemical aptasensor based on Ti3C2/Bi4VO8Br/TiO2 nanocomposite

ce:correspondence.ce:text: Corresponding author at: Key Laboratory of Modern Agriculture Equipment and Technology, School of Chemistry and Chemical Engineering, Jiangsu University, Zhenjiang 212013, PR China.

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: Key Laboratory of Modern Agriculture Equipment and Technology
School of Chemistry and Chemical Engineering

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: It is important to develop a selective and ultrasensitive detection of ciprofloxacin (CIP), owing to the relationship between milk security and human health. Herein, Ti3C2/Bi4VO8Br/TiO2 nanocomposites with new strategy were prepared. Based on the heterojunction formed between Bi4VO8Br and TiO2, the photogenerated electrons and holes complete the first step of transfer. Under the action of high conductivity Ti3C2, the electrons separated from the first step are further transferred to ITO electrode. The photocurrent intensity of Ti3C2/Bi4VO8Br/TiO2 nanocomposite was about 10 times that of the Ti3C2/Bi4VO8Br and 90 times that of Bi4VO8Br. The constructed PEC aptasensor presented a “on-off-on” detection signal and completed the specific detection of CIP in milk. With the increase of target detection concentration, the PEC aptasensor presented a detection range of 1–1500 nM and a detection limit of 0.3 nM (S/N = 3). The results are consistent with those of the national standard method.

ce:section.ce:para: Milk provides a complete nutrient source for human being, including 90% of water, different proportions of lipid and protein, small amounts of minerals and other micronutrients [1]. The benefits of milk and the economic growth of human beings have led to an increase in milk sales around the world [2]. Ciprofloxacin (CIP), a metabolite of enrofloxacin, are extensively used in animal husbandry practices for treatment and prevention of diseases as well as for promotion of growth and improving production efficiency [3]. However, inappropriate use of CIP by dairy breeders, resulting in the residues of CIP in milk. The CIP will cause severe allergic reactions, gastrointestinal tract diseases, chronic bacterial prostatitis and kidney failure in human beings [4,5]. Therefore, many countries, such as Russia and the United States, had established a maximum residue level (MRL) to monitor CIP levels in food. In European Union and Russia, the MRL of CIP is 100 ng/mL [6]. However, China's standards are stricter, and antibiotic residues should not be present in milk [7].

Up until now, different kinds of traditional methods were used to detect CIP, such as liquid chromatography-mass spectrometry [8], high performance liquid chromatography [9] and fluorescence detection technology [10]. Despite the fact that these methods can detect multiple substances at the same time and have high accuracy, they are limited in practical application due to their expensive instruments, long detection time and high detection cost [11]. At the same time, fluorescence detection technology is also tough to cut the mustard because of the complex facilities and tedious software [12]. In order to meet the requirement of quick, simple and low-cost detection for CIP, some new detection methods have been developed. For example, Zhou’s group presented an electrochemical luminescence (ECL) detection method [13]. This method realized the rapid detection of CIP, but the detection range and sensitivity need to be improved. Therefore, it is particularly important to develop a highly sensitive, simple and fast CIP detection method.

Photoelectrochemical (PEC) aptasensor is a detection platform based on electrochemistry and photochemistry, has been widely concerned in analytical performance and bioassay applications because of sensitive detection, short time-consuming and low-cost [14,15]. In the construction of PEC aptasensor detection platform, the choice of high-performance photoactive materials is the most basic part due to the appropriate photoactive materials can make the aptasensor with a more stable output signal to ensure the performance [16,17].

Bismuth-based materials is a photoactive material with great development potential because of unique layered structure, suitable band gap, non-toxic and harmless [18]. According to the reports, Bi4MO8X has been applied in the fields of photocatalytic degradation and water decomposition [19], and also shows a broad application prospect in the field of PEC aptasensor, but its application is still greatly limited owing to the slow electronic mobility and rapid recombination of electron-hole pairs [20]. In order to improve the photoelectric conversion efficiency of Bi4MO8X, researchers have tried strategies such as doping [21] and constructing Z-scheme [22], the photoelectric signal has been improved. In general, coupling Bi4MO8X with other conductors is an effective strategy [23]. Ti3C2 has been widely used in photocatalysis and photoelectrochemistry as conductor material [24]. According to our previous work reports, the high electrical conductivity of Ti3C2 can transfer photogenerated electrons to the ITO electrode so that the PEC performance has been improved [14]. Although this strategy improves the PEC performance, some drawbacks are still remained. The problem of rapid recombination of photogenerated electron-hole pairs has not been solved. Heterojunction, another strategy will be proposed. TiO2 is widely used in environmental pollution control and other fields because of its low cost, non-toxic, harmless, high stability and excellent oxidation performance, and is known as the most promising photocatalytic material [25]. It has been reported that the coupling of Bi-based materials and TiO2 to form heterojunctions can improve the efficiency of electron and hole separation [26]. Under the action of the combination of the two strategies, Bi4MO8X/TiO2 heterojunction effectively blocks the recombination of photogenerated electrons and holes, provides more electrons which are then transferred to ITO by Ti3C2. By combining the two strategies of heterojunction and conductivity, the photochemical properties have been improved greatly.

Herein, Taking Ti3C2/TiO2 as the precursor, Ti3C2/Bi4VO8Br/TiO2 was prepared by one-pot solvent thermal method. In Ti3C2/Bi4VO8Br/TiO2, a heterojunction is formed in Bi4VO8Br/TiO2 to conduct the first step of separation of photogenerated electrons and holes. Then, the separated photogenerated electrons are further transferred to the ITO electrode through the highly conductive Ti3C2, which effectively improves the separation efficiency of the photogenerated electrons and holes. The photocurrent was greatly improved and 90 times higher than pure Bi4VO8Br. After that, a PEC aptasensor based on Ti3C2/Bi4VO8Br/TiO2 for CIP detection was created, and showed high sensitivity, excellent specificity and distinguished repeatability.
The reagents and instruments used in this work were described in the Supplementary Information. Ti3C2 were prepared by an easy etching process according to the report [27].

Taking above Ti3C2 as the additive, 100 mg Ti3C2 and 0.1647 g NaBF4 were added to 15 mL diluted hydrochloric acid, stirred for 30 min, and transferred to 25 mL autoclaves for 12 h under the condition of 160 °C. After natural cooling to room temperature, the reactant was collected by vacuum filtration to obtain Ti3C2/TiO2 composite material.

Based on our previous preparation method of Ti3C2/Bi4VO8Br (You et al., 2021), Ti3C2/Bi4VO8Br/TiO2 nanocomposite was successfully synthesized, and the detailed process is shown in the supplementary information.

ITO was washed in boiled NaOH (1.0 mol/L) solution and then washed with ultrapure water and ethanol, separately. 20 μL of Ti3C2/Bi4VO8Br/TiO2 (2 mg/mL) droplets were coated on ITO electrode surface (0.5 cm2 square) and dried at room temperature for later use. Then, 20 μL of CIP aptamer (2 μM) was incubated on the Ti3C2/Bi4VO8Br/TiO2/ITO electrode. The surface of the aptamer/Ti3C2/Bi4VO8Br/TiO2/ITO electrode was cleaned with ultrapure water to remove the excess aptamers, and the target CIP in different concentrations was added to different electrodes, and incubated at 37 °C for 40 min. Finally, aptamer/Ti3C2/Bi4VO8Br/TiO2/ITO was used to construct the PEC aptasensor to complete the detection of CIP.

In order to explore the availability of the PEC aptasensor in actual milk samples, the detection of CIP in milk samples was completed. The Yili milk is purchased at the local trade market. Under magnetic stirring, equal volume (2 mL) of milk and PBS were mixed and stirring continuously for 10 min, then, 7 mL of ethyl acetate was added, stirring for another 15 min. The resulting solution was transferred to a centrifuge and centrifuged at 5000 rpm for 15 min to extract the supernatant. A further 7 mL of ethyl acetate was added and the process was repeated to collect the supernatant and the excess ethyl acetate was blown away by nitrogen. The final product was dissolved in PBS and different concentrations (5, 10, 15 nM) of CIP were added [28,29].

Scanning Electron Microscope (SEM) image was employed to describe the morphology of the prepared samples. Fig. S1A showed the morphology of Ti3C2/Bi4VO8Br, the Bi4VO8Br wafer was embedded in the Ti3C2 structure. And as shown in Fig. S1B, in addition to Bi4VO8Br combined with Ti3C2, the accordion-like Ti3C2 surface was sensitized with a layer of TiO2 nanoparticles, indicating that Bi4VO8Br, Ti3C2 and TiO2 were combined with in the composite material. To further elucidate the composition of Ti3C2/Bi4VO8Br/TiO2 nanocomposite samples, X-ray photoelectron spectroscopy (XPS) result was shown. The survey XPS spectrum (Fig. S2) fully demonstrates the exist of Bi, Br, C, O, Ti and V elements in Ti3C2/Bi4VO8Br/TiO2 nanocomposite. In Fig. 1 A, Bi 4f 7/2 peak located at 159.3 eV and Bi 4f 5/2 peak located at 165 eV, which were belong to Ti3C2/Bi4VO8Br/TiO2. Br 3D (Fig. 1B) and C 1s (Fig. 1C) belonging to the high binding energies of Ti3C2/Bi4VO8Br/TiO2, this indicates that the Ti3C2/Bi4VO8Br/TiO2 nanocomposite system has the interaction between Bi4VO8Br and Ti3C2 [30]. Lattice oxygen and adsorbed oxygen are embodied in Fig. 1D, which were shown in the peaks of 01s at 530 eV and 531.9 eV [31,32]. In Fig. 1E and F, the peaks of Ti 2P and V 2P are 466.5 eV and 524.6 eV respectively, indicating the exist of titanium and vanadium ions above XPS results confirm the successful synthesis of Ti3C2/Bi4VO8Br/TiO2.

Ti3C2/Bi4VO8Br/TiO2 and Ti3C2/TiO2 nanocomposite were researched by X-ray diffraction (XRD) in Fig. 2 A. Ti3C2/TiO2 XRD pattern is the same with the standard card of Ti3C2/TiO2, indicating that the composite material of Ti3C2/TiO2 has been successfully synthesized [33]. In the XRD pattern of Ti3C2/Bi4VO8Br/TiO2, the characteristic peaks of 30° and 55° belong to Bi4VO8Br, indicating the existence of Bi4VO8Br in the Ti3C2/Bi4VO8Br/TiO2 ternary composite material, that is, the ternary composite material was successfully prepared.

As shown in Fig. 2B, photocurrent signals of electrodes of different materials were explored. The photocurrent signals of Bi4VO8Br and Ti3C2 can be ignored (curves c and e). When Bi4VO8Br was combined with Ti3C2 and TiO2, the photocurrent signal was about 2.4 μA, 10 times that of Ti3C2/Bi4VO8Br composites, and significantly higher than 1.8 μA of Ti3C2/TiO2. This may be because the introduction of TiO2 further utilized sunlight, effectively promoted the generation of photogenerated electrons. On this basis, Ti3C2 can further transfer electrons to ITO and thus improved the photoelectric chemical properties. After more than 600 s of switching irradiation, the photocurrent response of Ti3C2/Bi4VO8Br/TiO2 nanocomposite decreased somewhat, but finally leveled off and could be used for the detection of actual samples.

Diffuse reflectance spectrum (DRS) was used to at investigate the light absorption capacity of Ti3C2/Bi4VO8Br/TiO2 and Bi4VO8Br nanocomposite. As showed in Fig. 3 A, compared with Ti3C2/Bi4VO8Br composite material and pure Bi4VO8Br, Ti3C2/Bi4VO8Br/TiO2 nanocomposite material has a wider absorption range in the visible light region, indicating that the absorption of Ti3C2/Bi4VO8Br/TiO2 material in the visible light range is enhanced to some extent. Therefore, it can be seen that the Ti3C2/Bi4VO8Br/TiO2 composite material effectively improves the visible light activity. In Fig. 3B, the band gap widths of Ti3C2/Bi4VO8Br/TiO2 composite, Ti3C2/Bi4VO8Br composite and Bi4VO8Br are 0.8 eV, 1.6 eV and 2.4 eV, respectively, indicating that the Ti3C2/Bi4VO8Br/TiO2 composite has a narrower band gap width, indicating that the composite is more likely to be excited by sunlight to generate more electric-hole pairs, thus improving the photoelectric chemical properties [34].

ce:section.ce:section-title: Fabrication of the PEC aptasensing platform
ce:section.ce:para: Fig. 4 A showed the photocurrent response of PEC aptasensor. Under the light, Ti3C2/Bi4VO8Br/TiO2/ITO revealed a higher photocurrent intensity (curve a), indicating that Ti3C2/Bi4VO8Br/TiO2 had a good photoelectric conversion efficiency. Aptamer/Ti3C2/Bi4VO8Br/TiO2/ITO photocurrent intensity (curve b) reduce the reason is because the CIP aptamer in electrode was modified, so as to increase the space resistance on the surface of the electrode [35,36]. The specific combination of the apperator sensor and 50 nM CIP improves the sensor response signal significantly (curve c). Therefore, the proposed PEC aptasensor detection platform can accurately measure CIP concentration.

Electrochemical Impedance Spectroscopy (EIS) characterization of the prepared detection platform further proves its successful construction. In Fig. 4B, the charge-transfer resistance of (Rct) Ti3C2/Bi4VO8Br/TiO2/ITO is about 700 ohms (curve a), when aptamer was attached to the Ti3C2/Bi4VO8Br/TiO2/ITO electrode (curve b) and modified, the Rct value increased from 700 ohms to 1700 ohms, indicating that aptamer was fixed on the surface of the Ti3C2/Bi4VO8Br/TiO2 nanocomposite successfully [35]. When 500 nM CIP was introduced, the Rct is significantly reduced to 1300 ohms due to the specific binding (curve c) of CIP and CIP aptamer. This is because the change of compound effectively promotes electron transfer, which leads to the reduction of Rct [17]. The equivalent circuit (Fig. 4B, inset) used for fitting the impedance data, where Rs, Rct, Zw and Q represented electrolyte solution resistance, electron transfer resistance, warburg impedance and constant phase elements, respectively. These results confirm that the preparation of PEC aptasensor has been succeed.

The process of PEC aptasensor used to detect CIP is shown in Scheme 1 . The PEC response mechanism of Ti3C2/Bi4VO8Br/TiO2/ITO was shown. Both of Bi4VO8Br and TiO2 produce photogenic electrons in visible light. Photogenerated electrons transition from the valence band (VB) to the conduction band (CB), leaving holes in the valence band, thus forming photogenerated electron-hole pairs. Compared with Bi4VO8Br, since the band position of TiO2 is more negative, the electrons in the conduction band of TiO2 will actively transfer to the conduction band of Bi4VO8Br, thus transferring to ITO electrode and generating current. At the same time, the hole in Bi4VO8Br's valence band will jump into TiO2′s valence band. Such a structure makes it easier to separate photogenerated electrons from holes, thus producing a more stable and efficient current than Bi4VO8Br. High conductivity Ti3C2 can promote the rapid transfer of photogenerated electrons to ITO electrode, further inhibiting the recombination of photogenerated electrons and holes, so as to further improve the photoelectric performance. When the aptamer was added to the sensor, the electron transfer rate decreases due to the steric hindrance enhancement [32], the photocurrent signal decayed rapidly. When CIP is introduced, the CIP molecule binds to the aptamer molecule specifically and the aptamer molecule was released from the PEC aptasensor platform, the steric hindrance decreases and the photocurrent signal rises [37].

As shown in Fig. 4C, when the concentration of aptamer increased from 0.5 to 3.0 μM, the photocurrent response showed a gradual downward trend. However, when the concentration reaches 2.5 μM, the photocurrent reaches the lowest point and no longer decreases with the change of the molecule concentration of the aptamer. This indicates that the concentration of the adapter on the electrode surface reaches saturation at this time, and 2.5 μM is also the best concentration for the construction of the adapter sensor. As shown in Fig. 4D, the photocurrent a tendency of attenuated gradually with the incubation time of aptamer. When the incubation time reached 40 min, the photocurrent signal did not weaken with the increase of incubation time. This indicates that the aptamer on the electrode surface reaches saturation state at this time, and 40 min is also the best time for aptamer incubation.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Analytical performance of the PEC aptasensor
ce:section.ce:para: Under the optimized concentration and incubation time of the aptamer, a PEC detection platform with high efficiency and low cost for CIP was prepared. Fig. 5 A showed the changing trend of photocurrent of PEC sensor with the increase of CIP molecular concentration. With the increase of CIP concentration, the photocurrent signal rises. Fig. 5B is the corresponding linear calibration curve of photocurrent changing with CIP concentration, including 1 nM to 1500 nM of linear range and 0.3 nM of detection limit (S/N = 3). Compared with other detection methods of CIP (Table S1), this method has a wider linear range and a lower detection limit, and has a good effect on CIP analysis.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Selectivity and stability of the PEC aptasensor
ce:section.ce:para: Several common antibiotics are used for anti-interference tests (OTC, CAP, KAN and STR) in order to inquire the selectivity of the PEC aptasensor. In the same experimental environment, the interferer concentration of 1000 nM and CIP concentration of 100 nM were detected simultaneously. The experimental results are shown in Fig. 5C, the photocurrent response of interferer is significantly lower than that of CIP, which indicates that the constructed sensor can specifically recognize CIP. At the same time, the stability of PEC adapter sensor is also investigated. In Fig. 5D, under the continuous test lasting 600 s, CIP/apatamer/Ti3C2/Bi4VO8Br/TiO2/ITO electrode does not show obvious photocurrent fluctuations, which indicates that it is with excellent stability. At the same time, the photocurrent response of the electrode did not change significantly stored at 4 °C for 15 days, indicating that the sensor has good reproducibility.

In order to test the authenticity of CIP detection by the sensor in actual samples, the test of CIP in milk has been explored. In Table S2, the recoveries were in the range of 96.8∼111.6% with the RSD in the range of 2.58∼5.14%. The results show that the adaptive sensor can be used to detect CIP in actual milk samples, and the detection results are reliable. At the same time, the detection results of this method were similar to those of HPLC-MS/MS, and the difference between the two methods was less than 10%, indicating that the detection results of this method were similar to those of HPLC-MS/MS [38].

In summary, Ti3C2/Bi4VO8Br/TiO2 was prepared by one- step hydrothermal method using Ti3C2/TiO2 as precursor. Because of the heterojunction of Bi4VO8Br and TiO2, the electrons are separated from the holes. After that, Ti3C2 further transfers photogenerated electrons into ITO. Further, the Ti3C2/Bi4VO8Br/TiO2 was used as the photoactive material based on the superb photocurrent signals. Based on the aptamer and target provided specific recognization, a high sensitivity and specificity “on-off-on” photoelectrochemical CIP aptasensor was developed. Because of the ideal detection performance, the aptasensor was successfully used for the highly sensitive detection of CIP in milk samples, the results were similar to HPLC-MS/MS method. Therefore, this phenomenon indicating that the PEC CIP aptasensor based on Ti3C2/Bi4VO8Br/TiO2 developed a new platform for highly sensitive and selective detection of antibiotics in foodstuff analysis.

Fuheng You: Writing - original draft. Zuorui Wen: Data curation. Ruishuang Yuan: Methodology. Ding Lijun: Formal analysis. Jie Wei: Investigation. Jing Qian: Supervision. Lingliang Long: Validation. Kun Wang: Writing - review & editing.

ce:conflict-of-interest.ce:para: The authors declare that they have no known competing financial interests or personal relationships that could have appeared to influence the work reported in this paper.

ce:acknowledgment.ce:para: We gratefully acknowledge the National Natural Science Foundation of China (Nos. 22174055, 21675066 and 21976071), the Project Funded by the Priority Academic Program Development of Jiangsu Higher Education Institutions (No. PAPD-2018-87), the Foundation of Key Laboratory of Sensor Analysis of Tumor Marker, Ministry of Education, Qingdao University of Science and Technology (No. SATM201807) and Graduate Research and Innovation Projects of Jiangsu Province (KYCX21_3389).

ce:section.ce:label: Appendix A
ce:section.ce:para: Supplementary data to this article can be found online at https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jelechem.2022.116285.

ce:section.ce:label: Appendix A
ce:section.ce:para:
The following are the Supplementary data to this article:
Supplementary data 1

sb:title.sb:maintitle: A sensitive biosensor of CdS QDs sensitized CdWO4-TiO2 composite for the photoelectrochemical immunoassay of β-lactoglobulin in the milk

ce:bib-reference.ce:source-text: C.G. Li, Q.Y. Zhu, H.Q. Chang, M. Jiang, S.D. Mao, Z.W. Chen, L. Kong, H.L. Liu, H.Y. Tian, J. Wang, A sensitive biosensor of CdS QDs sensitized CdWO4-TiO2 composite for the photoelectrochemical immunoassay of β-lactoglobulin in the milk, J. Electroanal. Chem. 905 (2022) 115964.

sb:title.sb:maintitle: Two-dimensional Janus monolayers of MoSSe as promising sensor towards selected adulterants compounds

ce:bib-reference.ce:source-text: P. Panigrahi, D. Jini, H. Bae, H. Lee, R. Ahuja, T. Hussain, Two-dimensional Janus monolayers of MoSSe as promising sensor towards selected adulterants compounds, Appl. Surf. Sci. 542 (2021) 148590.

sb:title.sb:maintitle: Highly selective electrochemical detection of ciprofloxacin using reduced graphene oxide/poly(phenol red) modified glassy carbon electrode

ce:bib-reference.ce:source-text: R. Chauhan, A.A.S. Gill, Z. Nate, R. Karpoormath, Highly selective electrochemical detection of ciprofloxacin using reduced graphene oxide/poly(phenol red) modified glassy carbon electrode, J. Electroanal. Chem. 871 (2020) 114254.

sb:title.sb:maintitle: Polyethylenimine@Fe3O4@carbon nanotubes nanocomposite as a modifier in glassy carbon electrode for sensitive determination of ciprofloxacin in biological samples

ce:bib-reference.ce:source-text: N.R. Jalal, T. Madrakian, A. Afkhami, M. Ghamsari, Polyethylenimine@Fe3O4@carbon nanotubes nanocomposite as a modifier in glassy carbon electrode for sensitive determination of ciprofloxacin in biological samples, J. Electroanal. Chem. 833 (2019) 281-289.

sb:title.sb:maintitle: Pollutant template method synthesis of oxygen vacancy and template cavity riched TB-TiO2@MFA towards selective photodegradation of ciprofloxacin
